title: SQL Antipatterns
subtitle: Avoiding the Pitfalls of Database Programming
themes: Recognizing and avoiding common SQL design mistakes that compound over time
Schema design as a long-lived, shared resource requiring judgment not just syntax
Referential integrity, foreign keys, and proper use of ON DELETE/UPDATE CASCADE
Hierarchical data modeling: nested set, closure table, and recursive CTE alternatives to naive trees
NULL as 'unknown' — avoiding comparison mistakes and silent logical errors
Type safety vs. flexibility trade-offs embodied by the Entity-Attribute-Value antipattern
Query performance: avoiding full-table operations like ORDER BY RAND() and unbounded COUNT(*)
SQL injection prevention through parameterized queries and the security stakes
awards: Pragmatic Bookshelf best-seller in Database category
Widely cited as the definitive practical guide to SQL design mistakes
Recommended reading by database teams at companies including Heroku, Pivotal, and Percona community
excerpt: SQL Antipatterns is the essential field guide to the most common and costly mistakes in SQL and database design. Bill Karwin identifies why seemingly reasonable decisions — from flat EAV tables to ORDER BY RAND() on millions of rows — silently degrade data quality, performance, and maintainability, then shows practical, vendor-neutral corrections.
